content: [{"section_id": "s01", "title": "Intermittent Fasting Protocols and Metabolic Syndrome Definitions", "description": "Characterizes the family of IF protocols (TRE, ADF, 5:2, FMD) and reviews the harmonized definition of metabolic syndrome as context for the review."}, {"section_id": "s02", "title": "Pooled Efficacy on Cardiometabolic Endpoints", "description": "Synthesizes meta-analytic effect sizes on fasting blood glucose, HbA1c, HOMA-IR, lipid profile, body weight, BMI, and blood pressure in metabolic-syndrome populations."}, {"section_id": "s03", "title": "Methodological Heterogeneity and GRADE Certainty", "description": "Examines sources of heterogeneity (protocol, duration, baseline BMI), publication bias, risk of bias, and the GRADE certainty levels assigned to key endpoints."}, {"section_id": "s04", "title": "Long-Term Safety and Observational Evidence", "description": "Addresses the long-term cardiovascular safety of IF, contrasting supervised RCT outcomes with observational cohort evidence and free-living Ramadan data."}, {"section_id": "s05", "title": "Biological Mechanisms and Pathways", "description": "Covers circadian alignment, metabolic-switch/autophagy, gut-immune signaling (IL-22/ILC3), and adipose tissue remodeling as candidate mechanisms of benefit."}, {"section_id": "s06", "title": "Clinical Implications and Research Priorities", "description": "Translates the evidence into patient-selection guidance and identifies the most important remaining research gaps."}]
